_note: MOCK procedure result. No real patient. For PreChart development/eval. Result of the urgent/emergent ERCP with biliary decompression this patient was referred for.

procedure.name: ERCP with sphincterotomy, stone extraction, and biliary stent placement
procedure.indication: Acute ascending cholangitis with suspected choledocholithiasis (CBD 12 mm, total bilirubin 6.2 mg/dL) — urgent/emergent biliary decompression
procedure.sedation: General endotracheal anesthesia given emergent status, sepsis, and aspiration risk
procedure.extent_reached: Second portion of duodenum; major papilla identified and cannulated; biliary tree opacified to the intrahepatic ducts

procedure_report: PROCEDURE: ERCP with biliary sphincterotomy, balloon stone extraction, and plastic biliary stent placement. INDICATION: acute cholangitis secondary to choledocholithiasis, emergent decompression. ANESTHESIA: general endotracheal (emergent, septic patient). PERIPROCEDURAL: Patient on apixaban 5 mg BID for atrial fibrillation with last dose today ~07:00 (near-peak effect). Per multidisciplinary discussion with anesthesia and pharmacy, given life-threatening cholangitis requiring emergent decompression, procedure proceeded without delay; apixaban held and 4-factor prothrombin complex concentrate (PCC) administered pre-procedure for reversal prior to sphincterotomy. Documented iodinated contrast reaction (hives) — premedicated with corticosteroids and antihistamine; low-osmolar contrast used with minimized volume. Broad-spectrum IV antibiotics and fluids continued. FINDINGS: The duodenoscope was advanced to the major papilla without difficulty. The papilla appeared bulging/edematous consistent with an impacted stone. The bile duct was deeply cannulated. Cholangiogram demonstrated a dilated common bile duct to 12 mm with a single obstructing 9 mm filling defect in the distal CBD and intrahepatic biliary dilation. A biliary sphincterotomy was performed with controlled cut; hemostasis was intact with no post-sphincterotomy bleeding. Balloon sweep of the duct extracted one pigmented stone with prompt drainage of purulent bile (frank pus), confirming cholangitis. Occlusion cholangiogram showed no residual filling defects. A 10 Fr x 7 cm plastic biliary stent was placed across the sphincterotomy to ensure ongoing drainage. IMPRESSION: Successful emergent biliary decompression — sphincterotomy, extraction of one CBD stone, drainage of purulent bile, and biliary stent placement. Technically successful. No immediate complication.

findings.finding: Bulging, edematous major papilla suggestive of impacted stone
Dilated common bile duct (12 mm) with single 9 mm obstructing distal CBD stone and intrahepatic biliary dilation
Purulent bile (frank pus) on duct drainage — confirms suppurative cholangitis
Occlusion cholangiogram with no residual filling defect
findings.action_taken: 10 Fr x 7 cm plastic biliary stent placement

specimens.type: aspirated bile for Gram stain and culture

pathology.actionable: Bile culture and sensitivities pending — narrow antibiotics when results return

disposition: Admitted to inpatient medicine/monitored bed post-procedure in stable condition. Continue IV antibiotics and fluids, sepsis monitoring. NPO advanced as tolerated. Serial post-ERCP monitoring for pancreatitis and delayed bleeding given periprocedural anticoagulation reversal.

followup.recommendation: Bile culture and sensitivities pending — tailor/de-escalate antibiotics when final results return
Repeat ERCP for biliary stent removal/exchange after cholangitis resolution
Referral for interval cholecystectomy for cholelithiasis (source of stones) once recovered from acute illness
Resume apixaban after confirming hemostasis and acceptable bleeding risk post-sphincterotomy; coordinate timing given atrial fibrillation stroke risk
Reconcile allergy list — add iodinated contrast reaction (hives); ensure premedication documented for future contrast studies
Monitor for post-ERCP pancreatitis and delayed post-sphincterotomy bleeding, heightened due to periprocedural anticoagulation

planted_followup_items.description: Plastic biliary stent placed at index ERCP requires scheduled removal/exchange after cholangitis resolves — retained stent risks occlusion, recurrent cholangitis, and stone/stent migration if dropped
planted_followup_items.expected_action: schedule follow-up ERCP for stent removal/exchange in 4-6 weeks and confirm patient is booked

planted_followup_items.description: Apixaban (atrial fibrillation, CHA2DS2-VASc 3) held and reversed with PCC for emergent sphincterotomy — resumption must be actively re-decided; failure to resume leaves patient at stroke risk, resuming too early risks post-sphincterotomy bleeding
planted_followup_items.expected_action: document explicit apixaban resumption plan with timing after hemostasis confirmed; coordinate GI and Cardiology

planted_followup_items.description: Bile Gram stain and culture/sensitivities pending from aspirated purulent bile
planted_followup_items.expected_action: review culture results and de-escalate/tailor antibiotics accordingly

planted_followup_items.description: Interval cholecystectomy needed for gallstone source to prevent recurrent choledocholithiasis and cholangitis
planted_followup_items.expected_action: place General Surgery referral for cholecystectomy after recovery from acute illness

planted_followup_items.description: Post-ERCP pancreatitis and delayed post-sphincterotomy bleeding watch — bleeding risk elevated by recent apixaban dose despite PCC reversal
planted_followup_items.expected_action: monitor symptoms, abdominal pain, lipase, hemoglobin, and stool over 24-48 hours

planted_followup_items.description: Iodinated contrast allergy (hives) confirmed on interview but absent from the allergy list; must be reconciled into the chart for future contrast-based studies
planted_followup_items.expected_action: add contrast allergy to allergy list and document required premedication

post_procedure_followup._note: Patient-reported stream after the procedure — the inbound signal the LeftBehind product triages. Each contact is labeled ground truth so triage can be scored.

contacts.verbatim: My throat is scratchy and I'm a little sore and gassy in my belly, but nothing sharp. Is that normal after the scope?
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-ERCP sore throat and mild insufflation-related abdominal soreness; no features of post-ERCP pancreatitis (no severe/persistent epigastric or back pain, no vomiting)
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; mild sore throat and transient bloating/soreness are expected and self-limited. Give return precautions for severe or worsening abdominal/back pain, fever, or vomiting (post-ERCP pancreatitis).
contacts.rationale: Benign expected post-endoscopy symptoms without red-flag features; safe to reassure with clear escalation criteria.

contacts.verbatim: The team held my blood thinner for the procedure. When am I supposed to start the apixaban again, and who tells me it's okay?
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient asking about resumption of held/reversed anticoagulation after sphincterotomy
contacts.expected_action: Answer from the plan: apixaban was held and reversed for the sphincterotomy; GI and Cardiology will set resumption timing (~48-72h) once hemostasis is confirmed. Counsel NOT to self-restart before the team confirms, given post-sphincterotomy bleeding risk.
contacts.rationale: Answerable from the follow-up plan; links to the med_resume item. Reinforces not restarting anticoagulation too early.

contacts.verbatim: Did the fluid you took from my bile duct grow anything? I'm still on the antibiotics and wanted to know if they'll change.
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient asking about pending bile culture results and antibiotic plan
contacts.expected_action: Answer from the result: bile culture/sensitivities pending (expected ~2026-07-21); the team will tailor or de-escalate antibiotics when final results return and will contact the patient with any change.
contacts.rationale: Answerable from the procedure result and follow-up plan; links to the pending_pathology item.
